title: rubus × carpinifoliiformis
intro: Rubus × carpinifoliiformis, also known as the hybrid blackberry, is a cross between the blackberry and the bramble. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × carpinifoliiformis include its potential to reduce the risk of heart disease, certain cancers, and cognitive decline, as well as its ability to support immune function and skin health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, with the latter often being used as a sweetener or flavoring agent, while the former two preparations are commonly used for medicinal purposes.
